parsed_review.summary: CP00-501 (RP16.P06.M05.S07-S16, risk A, 10 units, "Permission And Audit Binding") is a clean descriptor-only pack and is internally consistent across all artifacts. Implementation (registry.js pack binding + requirements, service.js pure/frozen descriptor & case-set functions, validators.js coverage + slice validators) matches the plan, the deep-equal-verified contract projection in governance-core-contract.json, the manifest, and command-evidence. Deliverable distribution (implementation 3, security_audit 2, ui 4, claude_review 1) and the 10 row titles match the baseline exactly. Tests (model.test.js:1441-1489) and the validator script use real fixtures with meaningful assertions and enforce the descriptor-only/no-write/authority boundaries. No write/network/exec/DB paths exist in src, the validator, or tests (tests are read-only). No-write/no-real-data attestations are extensive and coherent. Authority boundary holds: Claude is read-only and explicitly not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false), human final approval is required for runtime opening (CP00-502), and enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation. Hermes appears only as a deterministic descriptor evidence packet (receipt_shape descriptor_only..._no_write, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false) and is not embedded in product runtime. The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly bounded: manifest keeps production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, and the production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verified claim only — gates are not weakened. Review hardening is in place: forbidden_review_evidence encodes all CP178+ rejection criteria (auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields, final-approval/enterprise-trust claims), and the readiness auth-preflight passed with valid non-zero-byte JSON. The post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet present, which is expected because this review is the read_only_claude_run step that precedes them in the hardening sequence. No P0/P1/P2/P3 defects found; this review does not block pack or goal closeout.
